prompt:
Turn these notes into a LinkedIn post:
- Just wrapped a 3-month proof of concept with a financial services company
- They came in wanting to replace SailPoint but the real problem turned out to be that nobody owned access reviews — they had 6 different teams all doing them differently
- We ended up scoping OIG instead of full IGA replacement
- Key lesson: governance problems are usually people/process problems first, not technology problems
- The tech fix was actually the easy part

assertions.name: hook-leads-with-insight-not-story
assertions.description: Post opens with the lesson/insight, not the story setup ('Just wrapped a POC...'). The insight should be in the first 1-2 lines.

assertions.name: no-em-dash
assertions.description: Post body contains no em dashes (—)

assertions.name: ends-with-specific-question
assertions.description: Post ends with a specific, non-generic question

assertions.name: prose-not-bullets
assertions.description: Post uses prose paragraphs, not a numbered or bulleted list to convey the story

assertions.name: proper-hashtags
assertions.description: Includes 3-5 hashtags in # format at the end
